deep_dive.title: Less Experts, Faster Decoding: Cost-Aware Speculative Decoding for Mixture-of-Experts
deep_dive.abstract: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have become an important approach for scaling Large Language Models (LLMs), but their inference efficiency depends strongly on expert activation patterns. Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates autoregressive generation by verifying multiple draft tokens in parallel, yet existing draft selection strategies primarily optimize acceptance likelihood. In large-scale MoE models, however, selecting draft tokens also determines the union of experts activated during verification. We observe that confidence-driven SD can introduce \textit{expert scattering}: high-probability draft tokens may route to disjoint experts, increasing expert-weight memory traffic and reducing the speedup from speculation. Motivated by this observation, we revisit draft-tree selection under the non-uniform memory-cost structure of MoE inference. We propose \textsc{EcoSpec}, a cost-aware speculative decoding framework that incorporates predicted marginal expert activation cost into draft selection. With a lightweight expert predictor and a dynamic expert buffer, \textsc{EcoSpec} favors draft paths that preserve high acceptance likelihood while reusing experts already covered by the current verification set, without modifying the target-model verification rule. We evaluate \textsc{EcoSpec} on three large-scale MoE models, including DeepSeek-V3.1 (671B), Qwen3-235B-A22B, and GPT-OSS-120B, across reasoning, coding, question-answering, and dialogue benchmarks. \textsc{EcoSpec} consistently reduces active expert footprints and improves end-to-end decoding speed, achieving up to $1.62\times$ speedup. These results show that accounting for expert activation cost is important for efficient speculative decoding in large-scale MoE models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cost-aware speculative decoding for MoE LLMs to reduce expert scattering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码，减少专家分散
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码，减少专家分散
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码，减少专家分散
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes cost-aware draft selection for speculative decoding in Mixture-of-Experts LLMs to mitigate expert scattering and improve speedup.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 混合专家大语言模型的推理效率受专家激活模式影响，现有投机解码仅优化接受率，却可能引发专家分散，导致内存流量增加。本文提出成本感知的草稿选择策略，在考虑接受概率的同时控制激活专家数量，从而减少专家权重内存流量。实验结果表明，该方法在保持高接受率的同时显著提升加速比。该工作为MoE模型的投机解码提供了新的优化视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 混合专家大语言模型的推理效率受专家激活模式影响，现有投机解码仅优化接受率，却可能引发专家分散，导致内存流量增加。本文提出成本感知的草稿选择策略，在考虑接受概率的同时控制激活专家数量，从而减少专家权重内存流量。实验结果表明，该方法在保持高接受率的同时显著提升加速比。该工作为MoE模型的投机解码提供了新的优化视角。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: Unlocking Parallelism in Autoregressive Language Models via Speculative Decoding with Progressive Tree Drafting
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding has significantly accelerated Large Language Model (LLM) inference by alleviating memory-bound bottlenecks. However, traditional speculative decoding typically relies on auxiliary draft modules, incurring significant training and communication overhead. Although recent methods attempt to generate drafts within the target model itself, they often fail to fully exploit its latent parallel capacity due to a lack of structural coordination. In this paper, we propose \textbf{Progressive Tree Drafting (PTD)}, which employs a structured, guided parallel drafting strategy to harness the model's parallel potential. By coupling a progressive tree structure with a stepwise pruning mechanism, PTD actively guides the LLM to explore multiple semantic paths in a single forward pass, ensuring both draft diversity and coherence. Experiments demonstrate that PTD achieves up to $2\times$ decoding speedup across various benchmarks while remaining training-free and model-agnostic. Our code is available at: https://github.com/MINE-USTC/PTD.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculative decoding method for LLM with progressive tree drafting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过渐进式树草稿加速LLM推理的投机解码方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过渐进式树草稿加速LLM推理的投机解码方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过渐进式树草稿加速LLM推理的投机解码方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Progressive Tree Drafting (PTD) employs a structured guided parallel drafting strategy with stepwise pruning to harness LLMs parallel potential for faster speculative decoding.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统投机解码依赖辅助草稿模块，带来训练和通信开销。本文提出渐进式树草稿（PTD），通过耦合渐进树结构与逐步剪枝机制，在目标模型内部实现结构化并行草稿。实验表明，PTD显著加速LLM推理，且无需额外草稿模型。该方法充分利用了自回归模型的并行容量，为高效LLM推理提供了轻量级方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 传统投机解码依赖辅助草稿模块，带来训练和通信开销。本文提出渐进式树草稿（PTD），通过耦合渐进树结构与逐步剪枝机制，在目标模型内部实现结构化并行草稿。实验表明，PTD显著加速LLM推理，且无需额外草稿模型。该方法充分利用了自回归模型的并行容量，为高效LLM推理提供了轻量级方案。

deep_dive.title: D-cut: Adaptive Verification Depth Pruning for Batched Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates large language model (LLM) inference without compromising output quality. Recent parallel drafting methods further improve single-request performance by decoupling draft length from drafting latency, enabling longer drafts and higher mean accepted tokens (MAT). However, under high request concurrency, long drafts waste substantial computation on rejected tokens, increasing verification cost and potentially making speculative decoding slower than autoregressive decoding. We present D-Cut, an adaptive pruning method that selects draft tokens jointly across the batch and concentrates the verification budget on tokens most likely to be accepted. D-Cut is motivated by two observations. First, acceptance lengths vary considerably across concurrent requests; D-Cut therefore performs cross-request pruning, allocating the verification budget adaptively according to draft confidence. Second, verification cost depends strongly on the deployment environment, including GPU architecture and parallelism strategy; D-Cut incorporates a runtime cost model to adapt its pruning depth to the target environment. Experiments on dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) models show that, under high concurrency, D-Cut improves the average speedup from \(1.26\times\) to \(1.65\times\), restores acceleration in dense-model configurations where long-draft baselines are slower than autoregressive decoding, and achieves up to \(3.0\times\) speedup over autoregressive decoding on MoE models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adaptive verification depth pruning for batched speculative decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: D-Cut adaptively prunes draft tokens in batch speculative decoding to reduce wasted verification on rejected tokens, improving LLM inference throughput under high concurrency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码通过草稿-验证机制加速大模型推理，但在高并发场景下长草稿导致大量被拒token浪费计算。本文提出D-Cut，一种自适应批处理草稿剪枝方法，联合选择最可能被接受的token集中验证预算。实验表明，D-Cut在保持输出质量的同时显著减少验证开销，提升批量推理吞吐量，使投机解码在高并发下仍优于自回归解码。该工作为投机解码的实际部署提供了高效优化方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 投机解码通过草稿-验证机制加速大模型推理，但在高并发场景下长草稿导致大量被拒token浪费计算。本文提出D-Cut，一种自适应批处理草稿剪枝方法，联合选择最可能被接受的token集中验证预算。实验表明，D-Cut在保持输出质量的同时显著减少验证开销，提升批量推理吞吐量，使投机解码在高并发下仍优于自回归解码。该工作为投机解码的实际部署提供了高效优化方案。
